name: Subir mi Credito | Credit Points 720
description_short: Spanish-language credit repair service operating 10+ years, disputing inaccurate and unverifiable items on credit reports for consumers in the USA and Puerto Rico.
description_long:
Subir mi Crédito, operating under the brand name CP720 (Credit Points 720), is a credit repair company with over a decade of experience serving Spanish-speaking consumers across the United States and Puerto Rico. The company positions itself as a comprehensive credit repair solution designed to help families achieve their financial goals through improved creditworthiness.

The company offers credit dispute services that operate within the framework of the Credit Repair Organizations Act (CROA). Their service model involves three steps: initial assessment of the client's credit situation and financial goals, personalized recommendations and strategies for credit improvement, and dispute work aimed at removing inaccurate, obsolete, or unverifiable negative items from credit reports. They work directly with creditors and credit bureaus to identify violations and leverage those violations to request removal of disputed accounts.

Subir mi Crédito distinguishes itself primarily through bilingual/Spanish-language marketing and service delivery targeted at Latino consumers, a demographic often underserved by mainstream credit repair companies. They claim to have helped thousands of families and provide estimated timelines (3-6 months) and transparent initial pricing ($54.99 to start). Their FAQ section demonstrates legal awareness by explicitly stating CROA compliance limitations—they cannot remove accurate, current, or verifiable items, only those meeting specific legal criteria.

However, the company makes no performance guarantees. Their own FAQ explicitly warns that credit score increases are unpredictable due to multiple variables in credit scoring models and consumer behavior during the service period. They cannot promise specific account removals in advance. The service outcomes depend heavily on factors outside their control, and clients are responsible for maintaining good financial habits throughout the process.
pros: 10+ years of stated experience in credit repair services
Explicit CROA compliance in marketing—clearly states they can only remove inaccurate, obsolete, or unverifiable items
Low initial fee ($54.99) with costs scaled to number of negative accounts
Bilingual (Spanish/English) service targeting underserved Latino demographic
Operates in both USA and Puerto Rico
FAQ demonstrates legal sophistication and honest limitations of credit repair
cons: Makes no performance guarantees—cannot promise which accounts will be removed or by when
No transparent pricing disclosed beyond initial $54.99; final costs depend on account count and plan selection
No independent reviews, ratings, or third-party verification visible on website
Cannot control credit score increases, which depend on client behavior and credit scoring variables outside their scope
Requires significant personal documentation (ID, SSN copy, two proofs of address) creating privacy considerations
best_for: Spanish-speaking consumers with inaccurate or unverifiable negative items on their credit reports
Individuals in USA or Puerto Rico seeking credit repair without English-language barriers
Consumers who have been denied credit and want to understand and challenge errors on their credit files
People planning major financial purchases (home, auto) who need credit profile improvement first
diagnosis: Best for Spanish-speaking consumers in the US or Puerto Rico who have inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable items damaging their credit reports and want professional dispute assistance. Primary caveat: credit repair cannot remove accurate or verifiable negative items, credit score increases are unpredictable, and outcomes depend significantly on the consumer's financial behavior during the service period—realistic expectations about what credit repair can and cannot accomplish are essential.
services: Identification of inaccurate, obsolete, or unverifiable negative items
Dispute filing with credit bureaus on behalf of clients
Violation identification for leverage in disputes
Estimated timeline projections for service completion
Account removal assistance (collections, late payments, repossessions, student loans, public records)

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 330 SW 27th Ave #701, Miami, FL 33135, USA
